title: Tender Sweet Norwegian Pork Onigiri
description: A sweet and velvety Norwegian dish featuring pork shoulder, shaped into nigiri, perfect for a quick and low-fat friendly meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together brown sugar, soy sauce, rice vinegar, olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Add the pork shoulder to the bowl and coat with the sugar and soy sauce mixture.
Place the pork shoulder on a baking sheet and bake for 20 minutes.
Remove the pork from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes.
Slice the pork into thin strips.
Cut the norwegian flatbread into small squares.
Assemble the onigiri by placing a small amount of pork onto the center of each bread square.
Fold the bread over the pork to form a triangle and press the edges together to seal.
Sprinkle the tops with sesame seeds if desired.
Serve the onigiri warm or at room temperature.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F, then rest 3 minutes before serving.
